steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls after "world" inside the sentence: "the Forth Road Bridge...is one of the longest suspension bridges in the ______ the Yavuz Sultan Selim Bridge...is even longer." The left side is an independent clause; the right side is also an independent clause. The ideas contrast (one bridge ranks among the longest; another is longer). Two independent clauses in contrast must be joined with a comma + coordinating conjunction. "But" is the right conjunction for contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'world, but,': the extra comma after "but" is incorrect — a coordinating conjunction joining two independent clauses does not take a following comma. ✗
  • Choice B — 'world,': a comma alone joining two independent clauses is a comma splice, and it drops the contrast signal. ✗
  • Choice C — 'world, but': comma + "but" correctly joins two independent clauses with a contrast meaning. ✓
  • Choice D — 'world but': omitting the comma before "but" violates the rule that a coordinating conjunction joining two independent clauses requires a preceding comma. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
